title: analytics — per-provider batch defaults
description: Default batch_config values per provider. Applied when creating a new AnalyticsProviderConfig without an explicit batch_config. Tenants with analytics_batch_configuration entitlement may override.
note: Values chosen based on each provider's published batch limits + a reasonable flush interval that balances latency (want events dispatched soon) against efficiency (batch bigger = fewer HTTP calls). Non-batching providers (June) list `null` for size + flush.

posthog.notes: PostHog accepts up to 1000/batch. Choosing 500 is a conservative default that leaves headroom + ships events within 5s.

amplitude.notes: Amplitude accepts 1000/batch AND 10MB. We cap batch bytes at 5MB to keep the failure blast radius bounded.

mixpanel.notes: Mixpanel accepts 2000/batch. Higher default batch size than others because Mixpanel's /import endpoint is optimised for bulk. Longer flush interval (10s) because Mixpanel dispatch is typically background-analytics oriented, not real-time.

segment.notes: Segment's /v1/batch hard-caps at 100 events + 500KB. Longer flush interval accepts Segment's downstream fan-out latency.

june.notes: June does NOT support batching. One HTTP request per event. size/flush ignored.

google_analytics_4.notes: GA4 measurement protocol caps at 25 events per request. Tight cap.

custom_webhook.notes: Conservative defaults. Tenant-facing consumer can handle whatever the tenant configures.
